title: We Will Rock You School Edition
description: We Will Rock You School Edition follows two young rebels, Galileo and Scaramouche, in a dystopian future where individuality and musical instruments are outlawed.

title: Ralph Vaughan Williams’ A Sea Symphony performed by the East Bridge Chorale and Caritas Chamber Choir
description:
Ralph Vaughan Williams’ A Sea Symphony performed by the East Bridge Chorale and Caritas Chamber Choir

The first half of our Summer concert features the much loved orchestral work Hebrides Overture (“Fingal’s Cave”) by Felix Mendelssohn followed by Ralph Vaughan Williams’ Serenade to Music performed by Caritas Chamber Choir.

The second half features Ralph Vaughan Williams’ A Sea Symphony (Symphony No.1), a thrilling four-movement choral symphony for soprano, baritone, chorus and orchestra that captures the power, mystery, and beauty of the sea. It sets text from Walt Whitman’s poetry to explore the sea as a metaphor for life and eternity. It is a landmark work composed between 1903 and 1909, first performed in 1910, in which the choir plays a role equal to the orchestra.

Music Director: Benedict Preece

East Bridge Concert Orchestra, Alexandra Caldon

David Stout, Baritone; Rebecca Hardwick, Soprano

title: Cloud 9
description: Cloud 9 is a landmark play about sexual politics in colonial Africa and modern-day Britain, in which all our assumptions about sex and gender are stunningly exploded.

title: Canterbury Primary: Aladdin Jr
description:
Our talented Year 6 pupils are delighted to present Aladdin JR., a magical adventure filled with laughter, music and unforgettable characters.

Join Aladdin, Jasmine and the Genie as they embark on a journey of friendship, courage and wishes come true. Packed with energy, creativity and fantastic performances, this special production showcases the hard work and enthusiasm of our young cast. We look forward to welcoming you to an enchanting evening.

title: Treeline
description:
Treeline

Presented by Graham Fitkin

Treeline is a unique European musical and cycling venture joining countries and communities together on a 2500-mile arboreal journey.

Devised by maverick composer Graham Fitkin, this concert tour focuses on trees and biodiversity and has been produced with communities in ten different countries. Creative work has been produced from communities along the route. Data and sounds from specific trees from the Carpathian Mountains to Epping Forest are woven into this extraordinary new musical work.

Join us as Graham previews his amazing and important Treeline project, ahead of the concert in November. Graham will play some excerpts from the piece, explain the technology and talks about how he records trees and uses their sounds within the music.

The event will include a Q&A with Graham.

More details

Production details

Treeline

Mon 20 July

title: Illuminate – Next Generation Dance Studio
description: Join the pupils of Next Generation Dance Studio for an enchanting showcase, ‘Illuminate’, where music, movement, and creativity blend into a dazzling world of light and colour.
